titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Leonard Strong Collection
titlestmt.author: Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11870851.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 03:04

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Leonard Strong Collection consists of autograph manuscripts and typescripts of poems, essays, short stories, and monographs; correspondence; and drawings by Strong which were acquired by the library over several decades. A group of materials acquired from James T. Babb include his correspondence with Strong and the book and manuscript dealer Bertram Rota.

persname.part: Strong, L. A. G. (Leonard Alfred George), 1896-1958

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of James T. Babb, 1965; purchased from G. F. Sims on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1973; and acquired from unidentified sources.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing, in 2014.
The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as Uncat MS Vault Strong and Uncat MS Vault 159.

bioghist.head: Leonard Strong (1896-1958)
bioghist.p: Leonard Alfred George Strong was an English writer of essays, novels, poetry, history, and other works. He was a director of Methuen Ltd. publishers from 1938 until 1958. Green Memory , Strong's autobiography, was published by Methuen Ltd. in 1961.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Leonard Strong Collection consists of autograph manuscripts and typescripts of poems, essays, short stories, and monographs; correspondence; and drawings by Strong which were acquired by the library over several decades. A group of materials acquired from James T. Babb include his correspondence with Strong and the book and manuscript dealer Bertram Rota.
A folder of catalog cards and lists for the Leonard Strong Collection is filed in box 1.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in three groupings: I. December 1965 Acquisition. II. August 1973 Acquisition. III. Undated Acquisitions.

persname.part: Strong, L. A. G. (Leonard Alfred George), 1896-1958

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of James T. Babb's gift of Strong material which includes Babb's correspondence with Strong and his wife, Sylvia; Strong's outgoing correspondence with friends and colleagues; and manuscripts and typescripts of essays, poems, short stories, and notes. The material also includes Babb's correspondence with the dealer Bertram Rota, from whom he acquired his collection, and the artist J. Howard Lewis.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault 159.

did.unittitle: Leonard Strong Collection card catalogs and lists
Essays, poems, short stories, and notes
"A [autobiographical] Note by L. A. G. Strong"
Broadcast introduction to the English Festival of Spoken Poetry

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault Strong.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of an autograph manuscript for Strong's first book, Dewer Rides , first published in London by V. Gollancz Ltd. in 1929.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault Strong.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of bound and unbound manuscripts and typescripts of essays and monographs.

did.unittitle: "The Hansom Cab and the Pigeon: Random Reflections Upon the Silver Jubilee of King George the Fifth"
